rulings.Case Title: LARRY E. ROSENBERGER ET AL VS. MICHAEL FERGUSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Notice Of Hearing
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 3, 2021, Line 1, PLAINTIFFs LARRY ROSENBERGER and DIANE ROSENBERGER's Motion for assignment order, restraining order and miscellaneous relief: Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted as requested. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to williamclynn@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MAGNOLIADRHOMES LLC VS. PEAK FINANCIAL COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling) Plaintiffs Magnoliadrhomes Llc And Youlin Wang Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One) And Requests For Production And To Compel Compliance With Requests For Production
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 3, 2021, Line 7, (Part 2 Of 2 For Tentative Ruling) (Tentative ruling continued from previous entry.) Plaintiffs Magnoliadromes LLC And Youlin Wang's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One) And Requests For Production And To Compel Compliance With Requests For Production As to Defendant Blueprint, the Court finds that Defendant Blueprint's Supplemental Responses to Form Interrogatories-General No. 15.1 (as to each denial of a material allegation and each affirmative defense) to be woefully deficient, contains improper objections, and is not code-compliant (without verification). Defendant Blueprint's responses to 15.1 did not properly identify each denial of a material allegation and did not provide responses required by No. 15.1(a-c) and appear to be non-responsive as to each affirmative defense since the responses appear to be from Defendant Libkey and not Defendant Blueprint. The Court also finds that Defendant Blueprint's Supplemental Response to Request for Production of Documents Set One contains improper objections and is not code-compliant (without verification). The Court is inclined to grant the motion and order defendants to serve code-compliant, amended/supplemental responses. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to williamclynn@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MAGNOLIADRHOMES LLC VS. PEAK FINANCIAL COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Mtc & Mpa
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 3, 2021, Line 7, (part 1 of 2 for tentative ruling) PLAINTIFFs MAGNOLIADRHOMES LLC AND YOULIN WANG's Motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories (set one) and requests for production and to compel compliance with requests for production Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. The Court first notes that moving party improperly combined six (6) motions in one. Nevertheless, as to Defendant Peak, the Court finds that Defendant Peak's Supplemental Responses to Form Interrogatories-General Nos. 3.1 (no response to a, c, d, e) and 15.1 (as to each denial of a material allegation and each affirmative defense) to be woefully deficient, contains improper objections, and is not code-compliant (without verification). Defendant Peak did not provide any response to No. 3.1(a, c-e) and its responses to 15.1 did not properly identify each denial of a material allegation and did not provide responses required by No. 15.1(a-c) and appear to be non-responsive as to each affirmative defense since the responses appear to be from Defendant Libkey and not Defendant Peak. The Court also finds that Defendant Peak's Supplemental Response to Request for Production of Documents Set One contains improper objections and is not code-compliant (without verification). As to Defendant Libkey, the Court finds that Defendant Libkey's Supplemental Responses to Form Interrogatories-General No 15.1 to be deficient, contains improper objections, and is not code-compliant (without verification). Defendant Libkey's original and supplemental responses to 15.1 did not properly identify each denial of a material allegation and did not provide comply No. 15.1(a-c) . The Court also finds that Defendant Libkey's Supplemental Response to Request for Production of Documents Set One contains improper objections and is not code-compliant (without verification). (tentative ruling continued, see next entry) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 3, 2021, Line 8, PLAINTIFF JANE DOE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Certain Affirmative Defenses Raised By Defendant Brock Aguirre. Plaintiff's motion for summary adjudication as to the defenses for Apportionment of Fault, Comparative Fault, Estoppel, Unclean Hands, Consent, and California Civil Code 1431.2(a) [apportionment of fault] is granted. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to all counsel) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Monday, May 3, 2021, Line 8, DEFENDANT JOHN MUNGUIA's Amended Notice Of Hearing Re Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Written Discovery, Compliance With Demand For Documents, Service Of Verifications, Monetary Sanctions Originally Filed On February 24, 2021 Pro Tem Judge William Lynn, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: INTERNATIONAL WALLS, INC. VS. ALLIED WORLD ASSURANCE COMPANY (U.S.) INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, May 3, 2021, Line 12, 3. DEFENDANT ALLIED WORLD ASSURANCE COMPANY (U.S.) INC.'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. The verified application of Anna J. Schaffner to appear as counsel pro hac vice to defendant is granted. No opposition filed, and the application conforms to Cal. R. Ct. 9.40. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing. =(302/EPS)
